summary: Added repository contribution guidance for AI-assisted decompilation work and introduced a minimal pull request template that directs contributors to the contributing guidelines before opening a PR. The AI policy permits AI use for matching functions but explicitly discourages AI-generated struct field/global identifier names, data-section matching attempts, AI output in PR or issue bodies/comments, and automated code reviews.
what_changed: Stated that using AI to match functions is acceptable and frequently practiced.
Added guidance to avoid letting AI name struct fields or other globally accessible identifiers.
Added guidance to discourage AI from attempting to match data sections, keeping AI assistance focused on code matches.
Added guidance not to include AI output in PR or issue bodies/comments and not to submit automated code reviews.
Allowed concise, relevant AI summaries only if visually distinguished from human writing by wrapping them in markdown quote syntax.
Added `.github/pull_request_template.md` with a one-line reminder to read the contributing guidelines before submitting a pull request.
smart_moves: Centralized AI-use expectations in the contributing guide rather than scattering them across review comments or informal Discord guidance.
Allowed AI assistance for code matching while drawing stricter boundaries around naming and data sections, two areas where low-confidence generated output can cause long-lived project-wide confusion.
Used the pull request template as a lightweight enforcement mechanism by making every PR author see a link to the contributing guidelines before submission.
Distinguished acceptable AI summaries from human-authored review discussion by requiring markdown quote formatting, reducing ambiguity about provenance.
decomp_lessons: AI may be useful for function matching, but generated names for struct fields and globally accessible identifiers should not be trusted without human project knowledge.
Data-section matching appears to require special care in this project; this PR explicitly tells contributors to keep AI focused on code matches rather than data sections.
Repository-level process documentation can encode decompilation norms that would otherwise be repeated in reviews.
PR templates can steer new contributors toward current matching, naming, and review expectations before reviewers need to intervene.
naming_conventions: Do not allow AI to name fields in structs.
Do not allow AI to name other globally accessible identifiers.
The PR provides policy guidance but does not introduce new symbol, function, or struct naming schemes.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: AI-assisted function matching is explicitly acceptable.
AI should be discouraged from attempting to match data sections.
AI assistance should focus solely on code matches according to the new guideline.
No specific assembly technique, compiler tactic, or matching workaround was introduced in the diff.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or formal reviews were present in the provided PR context.

The merged diff itself acts as process guidance for future reviews: reviewers can point contributors to the AI Assistance section when AI-generated names, data-section attempts, raw AI output, or automated review comments appear.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that reference the AI Assistance contributing guidelines.
Find review comments asking contributors not to use AI-generated names for struct fields or globals.
Find PRs where AI was used for function matching but not data-section matching.
Find changes to `.github/CONTRIBUTING.md` after PR 2513 that refine AI policy.
